summary: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) attacked a Singapore-flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz, according to U.S. officials. The IDF is preparing for anticipated mass protests against Hamas across the Gaza Strip tomorrow, while a senior American source reports Israel will withdraw from parts of southern Lebanon and cede control to the Lebanese Armed Forces. Domestically, Iran's execution rate has accelerated since the ceasefire with the U.S. and Israel, with 103 executions documented in the first 23 days of the current month.
key_points: U.S. officials report the IRGC attacked a Singapore-flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz today. (Source: @naya_foriraq/79887)

The IDF is preparing for mass protests against Hamas expected tomorrow across the Gaza Strip, assessing the possibility of friction events or Hamas attempts to exploit the situation. (Source: @inon_yttach/9167)
A senior American source tells Reuters that Israel will withdraw from parts of southern Lebanon and armed Lebanese army forces will take control of those areas. (Source: @pkpoi/142966)

Iran's execution rate has accelerated since the ceasefire, with 103 executions documented in the first 23 days of the current month, up from at least 74 the previous month. (Source: @VahidOnline/76662)

An IDF reserve soldier, Master Sgt. Basil Sweid, 32, was killed in an operational accident in southern Lebanon when his refueling truck overturned in the Rab al-Thalathine area. (Source: @manniefabian/58677)

The U.S. Senate voted 50-47 to block a Democratic-led resolution that would have required congressional authorization for military action against Iran, effectively halting the measure. (Source: @VahidOnline/76660)
Israeli President Herzog's helicopter made an emergency landing at Palmachim base after a bird struck the aircraft en route to Shavei Shomron. (Source: @amitsegal/62541)
An Israeli Arab resident of Umm al-Fahm was arrested on suspicion of serious security activity, with two M16 rifles, a Carlo-type weapon, and magazines found in his home. (Source: @inon_yttach/9166)

sections.executive_summary: Iran's IRGC attacked a Singapore-flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz, marking a significant escalation in maritime threats. The IDF is preparing for anticipated mass protests against Hamas across the Gaza Strip tomorrow, while a senior American source reports Israel will withdraw from parts of southern Lebanon and cede control to the Lebanese Armed Forces. Domestically, Iran's execution rate has accelerated since the ceasefire, with 103 executions documented in the first 23 days of the current month.

points: The IRGC attacked a Singapore-flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz today, according to U.S. officials. (Source: @naya_foriraq/79887)

Iran's senior negotiator, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, rejected U.S. claims that Iran would spend unfrozen assets on American agricultural products, calling the assertion a 'lie' and stating the only harvest from U.S. actions is 'decades of distrust.' (Source: @VahidOnline/76664)

The U.S. Senate blocked a Democratic-led resolution on war powers regarding Iran, voting 50-47 to halt the measure after Senators Rand Paul and Bill Cassidy changed their positions. (Source: @VahidOnline/76660)

Iran's execution rate has accelerated since the ceasefire with the U.S. and Israel, with 103 executions documented in the first 23 days of the current month, up from at least 74 the previous month. (Source: @VahidOnline/76662)

U.S. Energy Secretary stated that lifting sanctions on Iranian oil would allow Iran to sell in other markets and receive payment in dollars, but that unfrozen funds would be subject to strict oversight on spending. (Source: @naya_foriraq/79882)
The UAE, described as the most pro-Israel Gulf state, is conducting unprecedented talks with Iran, including with the IRGC, according to U.S. Senator JD Vance. (Source: @amitsegal/62540)

The IDF is preparing for mass protests against Hamas expected tomorrow across the Gaza Strip, assessing the possibility of friction events or Hamas attempts to exploit the situation. (Source: @inon_yttach/9167)
A senior American source tells Reuters that Israel will withdraw from parts of southern Lebanon and armed Lebanese army forces will take control of those areas. (Source: @pkpoi/142966)

An IDF reserve soldier, Master Sgt. Basil Sweid, 32, from Peki'in, was killed in an operational accident in southern Lebanon when his refueling truck overturned in the Rab al-Thalathine area around 11 p.m. last night. (Source: @manniefabian/58677)
Israeli President Herzog's helicopter made an emergency landing at Palmachim base after a bird struck the aircraft en route to Shavei Shomron; no injuries reported. (Source: @amitsegal/62541)
An Israeli Arab resident of Umm al-Fahm was arrested on suspicion of serious security activity, with two M16 rifles, a Carlo-type weapon, and magazines found in his home. (Source: @inon_yttach/9166)

The IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir stated the military needs every combat soldier and commander following threats by rabbis to stop sending students to serve in integrated tank units. (Source: @manniefabian/58681)

The IDF is preparing for mass protests against Hamas expected tomorrow across the Gaza Strip, assessing the possibility of friction events or Hamas attempts to exploit the situation. (Source: @inon_yttach/9167)

The IDF Gaza Brigade is increasing vigilance ahead of the possibility that Hamas will try to confront protesters or exploit the situation for friction events with Israel, including crossing the yellow line. (Source: @inon_yttach/9167)
Israeli military source stated the protests appear authentic but it is uncertain what will happen in practice. (Source: @inon_yttach/9167)
A senior American source tells Reuters that Israel will withdraw from parts of southern Lebanon and armed Lebanese army forces will take control of those areas. (Source: @pkpoi/142966)

An IDF reserve soldier, Master Sgt. Basil Sweid, 32, was killed in an operational accident in southern Lebanon when his refueling truck overturned in the Rab al-Thalathine area around 11 p.m. last night. (Source: @manniefabian/58677)

An Israeli Katz stated Israel will not allow the return of civilian populations and that the first line of villages within the security zone have been completely destroyed. (Source: @rodast_omiddana/74345)
Lebanon reported an airstrike on a motorcycle between the towns of Zawtar and Mifdoun in the south of the country. (Source: @asafroz/50731)
Hostilities are currently ongoing in Kounine Beit Yahoun in Lebanon. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/372835)

Italy and France are launching a new coalition to support Lebanon once the current UNIFIL mandate expires at year's end, with plans for a potential international conference. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4336)

A joint Gulf-American statement condemned attacks by Iran-backed factions in Iraq against Gulf states, stating peace in the region requires confronting Iranian threats including missiles, drones, and support for proxies. (Source: @naya_foriraq/79879)

Iraq is considering leaving OPEC if its oil production quota is not significantly expanded, with an Iraqi oil ministry senior official telling Reuters that Iraq will be forced to consider all available options. (Source: @asafroz/50724)

Iraqi sources stated the option to withdraw from OPEC has already been considered but the decision at this stage is to remain and push for a higher quota, as Baghdad faces a severe economic crisis following the war with Iran and damage to its oil export capacity. (Source: @asafroz/50724)
U.S. officials report the IRGC attacked a Singapore-flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz today. (Source: @naya_foriraq/79887)

A joint Gulf-American statement condemned attacks by Iran-backed factions in Iraq against Gulf states, stating peace in the region requires confronting Iranian threats including missiles, drones, and support for proxies. (Source: @naya_foriraq/79879)
The UAE, described as the most pro-Israel Gulf state, is conducting unprecedented talks with Iran, including with the IRGC, according to U.S. Senator JD Vance. (Source: @amitsegal/62540)
57 ships transited the Strait of Hormuz in the last two days, carrying approximately 1,100 sailors and crew, according to UN data published by Reuters. (Source: @asafroz/50723)

sections.threat_assessment: The IRGC's attack on a Singapore-flagged vessel in the Strait of Hormuz represents a direct threat to international maritime security and freedom of navigation, potentially drawing further U.S. military presence to the region. The anticipated mass protests in Gaza against Hamas introduce an unpredictable variable that could trigger renewed Israeli military operations if friction events occur. The reported planned Israeli withdrawal from parts of southern Lebanon to Lebanese Armed Forces control suggests a potential de-escalation pathway, though the destruction of villages in the security zone indicates a hardened defensive posture. Iran's accelerated domestic executions signal internal regime consolidation amid external pressure.
sections.regional_response: The joint Gulf-American statement condemning Iran-backed attacks on Gulf states demonstrates continued alignment between Washington and Gulf capitals against Iranian proxy threats. The UAE's unprecedented talks with Iran, including with the IRGC, suggest a parallel diplomatic track that may reflect Gulf states' hedging strategies amid shifting U.S. policy. Italy and France's new coalition to support Lebanon ahead of UNIFIL's mandate expiration indicates European efforts to fill potential security vacuums in the region. Iraq's potential OPEC withdrawal threat reflects the economic strain the Iran conflict has placed on Baghdad's oil export capacity.
sections.intelligence_notes: The U.S. Senate's 50-47 vote to block the Iran war powers resolution, with Rand Paul abstaining at Trump's request, reveals the administration's strategy of maintaining maximum leverage in ongoing negotiations with Tehran. The IDF's assessment that Gaza protests appear 'authentic' but with uncertain outcomes suggests limited intelligence on Hamas's internal decision-making regarding crowd control. The reported Israeli plan to cede southern Lebanon positions to the Lebanese Armed Forces, if confirmed, would represent a significant shift from direct occupation to proxy security management. Iran's rejection of U.S. claims regarding unfrozen asset usage, combined with the accelerated execution rate, indicates the regime is prioritizing internal control over diplomatic concessions despite the ceasefire.
